prerequisites: A SmartReach.io account with access to Team Settings (the API key and team ID are generated under Settings -> Team Settings -> Integrations).
An API key generated from the Integrations page (keys are prefixed with uk_ and carry the permissions of the user who created them).
Your team ID (prefixed with team), since API operations are scoped to a specific team and SmartReach users may belong to multiple teams.

authMethods.name: API key (X-API-KEY header)
authMethods.summary: SmartReach authenticates requests with an API key passed in the X-API-KEY HTTP header as the raw key (no Bearer scheme). Keys are prefixed with uk_ (user key). Provide this value as the connector's api_key field. The key is created under Settings -> Team Settings -> Integrations in the API Key section.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to SmartReach.io and go to Settings -> Team Settings -> Integrations. In the API Key section, create an API key. The generated key is prefixed with uk_. Keep it secure, as it carries your account permissions; regenerating it can disrupt existing integrations.

setupSteps.title: Find your team ID
setupSteps.body: On the same Settings -> Team Settings -> Integrations page (API Key section), locate your team ID. It is prefixed with team and identifies which SmartReach team your API calls operate against.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key in the api_key field and your team ID in the teamid field. Both are required. The connector authenticates by sending the key in the X-API-KEY header and passing the team ID as the team_id query parameter against the base URL https://api.smartreach.io/api/v3.

sources.title: SmartReach API - Making an API call
